Off Calendar Sep-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Guy Stilson, 415.398.0200 fir Defendants Alvin Blaum et al.
Kristen Drake, 888.510.7511 for Traci Grace.
Marc Derewetzky, 925.288.1776 for Defendant Progressive Property Group.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE PROPERTY GROUP, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendant Progressive Property Group, Inc. For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues

In light of the opposition and new theories advanced by the Plaintiff coupled with apparent abandonment of some currently asserted claims (e.g. claims pertaining to unit 16), the Court will treat this motion as a Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings and will give Plaintiff a final opportunity to amend to allege viable tenancy-related novel claims arising out of purported tenancy-at-sufferance. Such claims must be made in good faith. It further appears that Plaintiff references a possible claim for breach of contract re: confidentiality agreement. If that is Plaintiff's intent, to assert an independent breach of contract cause of action, Plaintiff shall have leave to amend to so allege. Leave to amend is limited to any tenancy-related causes of action arising out of occupancy of unit 2 and breach of confidentiality agreement cause of action, if any. 30 days to file an amended complaint. Both sides to submit proposed orders. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
